jx_field_harvester.identified_role: harvests JX auth/session fields and publishes them into the cached globals consumed by the post-login auth scheduler
key_observations: Fetches JX_DISPLAY_NAME first and routes it through 0x1401303A0 rather than storing it in the OAuth/game-session cache globals.
Fetches JX_ACCESS_TOKEN, JX_REFRESH_TOKEN, JX_SESSION_ID, and JX_CHARACTER_ID through the imported helper at 0x140802740.
Uses those harvested values to populate the cached globals later consumed by the shield/oauth and game-session request builders.

field_fetch_order.notes: Persisted into the display/profile-like sink around client +0xB70 rather than the OAuth/game-session cache block.

evidence: 0x14015CC20 harvests JX_ACCESS_TOKEN immediately before the branch that copies the resulting string into the 0x140E58AA0 slot.
0x14029A3D0 later pairs 0x140E58AA0 with the literal token field name when building shield/oauth/check_token.
0x14015CC20 harvests JX_REFRESH_TOKEN immediately before the branch that copies the resulting string into the 0x140E58AB8 slot.
0x14029A0D0 consumes 0x140E58AB8 under the refresh_token field name when building shield/oauth/token.
0x14015CC20 harvests JX_SESSION_ID into a dedicated local string object and later copies that object into 0x140E58AD8 through 0x140031B10.
0x140299A30 checks 0x140E58AD8 for non-emptiness as one half of the gate that enables the later game-session path.
0x14015CC20 harvests JX_CHARACTER_ID into a second dedicated local string object and later copies that object into 0x140E58AF0 through 0x140031B10.
0x14029AD20 pairs 0x140E58AF0 with the accountId literal while building the game-session token request.

request_dispatch_scheduler.identified_role: auth-state scheduler that chooses the next shield/oauth or game-session request based on the cached JX fields and timer state

decision_order.meaning: Both JX_SESSION_ID and JX_CHARACTER_ID caches are populated, so the branch proceeds into the game-session token request.
No cached refresh/check timestamp is present, so the branch starts with shield/oauth/check_token.

decision_order.gate: current time exceeds the cached threshold derived from 0x140E58AD0
decision_order.meaning: The branch moves into shield/oauth/token refresh handling after the cached delay/expiry window elapses.
The branch falls through to a lightweight maintenance/reset helper instead of issuing a new refresh request.

timer_notes: 0x140E58AD0 behaves like a cached timestamp or next-eligible-refresh marker rather than a generic pointer.
0x14029A640 updates 0x140E58AD0 from response-derived state and the scheduler compares the current time against that stored value with an additional +0x3C offset.

check_token_builder.summary: Validates the cached JX_ACCESS_TOKEN through the shield/oauth/check_token endpoint.

refresh_builder.summary: Refreshes the OAuth token state using the cached JX_REFRESH_TOKEN and previously cached request metadata.

field_mapping.notes: The paired pointer resolves to the token_type_hint literal.

revoke_builder.summary: Builds a shield/oauth/revoke request that is closely tied to the cached refresh-token state.

game_session_builder.summary: Builds the later game-session token request using the cached JX_CHARACTER_ID as the accountId-bearing input.

cache_reset_helper.identified_role: clears the leading cached auth string/status state and conditionally tails into the revoke/request-reset path
key_observations: Copies an empty string into 0x140E58A80 and resets 0x140E58A98 to -2.
If the cached access-token slot at 0x140E58AA0 is non-empty after that reset, it jumps into 0x14029A900.

updated_conclusion: The login-result-0x3F continuation now resolves into a concrete cached-JX scheduler. JX_ACCESS_TOKEN and JX_REFRESH_TOKEN drive shield/oauth/check_token, shield/oauth/token, and revoke handling, while JX_SESSION_ID plus JX_CHARACTER_ID gate the later game-session/v1/tokens request.
next_targets: Resolve the remaining earlier cache block at 0x140E58B08..0x140E58B80 with the same level of precision now achieved for 0x140E58AA0..0x140E58AF0.
Prove whether the unresolved fifth continuation string is an upstream precursor to JX_DISPLAY_NAME or a distinct profile/session label that merely shares the same sink.
Identify the exact semantic content of the client strings at +0x19D88 and +0x19E30 that feed the earlier 0x140299C20 cache snapshot.
